condition:
In the rendered image the assembly stands vertically, roughly 800 mm tall and 277 mm across, with a flat disc plate at the top, a long thin rod descending from it, and a barrel-shaped cage of slats at the lower end. The grounded part is the disc plate (180 mm diameter × 10 mm thick, one central 10 mm through-hole), which serves as the topmost anchor. The rod is a featureless solid cylinder of 10 mm diameter and 500 mm length (aspect ratio 50) that passes through the disc plate's central bore; hole inference confirms this link with zero perpendicular misalignment and 0.3 mm axial overlap. At the lower end of the rod, a flanged bushing (56.5 mm outer flange diameter, 40 mm through-bore, 11.25 mm tall) and a bushing/locating plug (20 mm main-body radius, 23 mm rim radius, 77 mm tall with a conical lower taper and a blind 24 mm bore at the top) are linked coaxially through a second hole inference edge (diameter 40 mm, zero perpendicular misalignment, 11.85 mm axial overlap matching the bushing's full height). These hub components sit at the top of the cage. A splined ring (200 mm diameter, 3.9 mm thick, 40 external teeth, inner bore ~160 mm) encircles the hub zone; its 40 teeth match the 40 vane slats in count. Each vane slat is an extremely thin panel (~3.9 × 50 × 300 mm, aspect ratio 76.9) with a gentle outward curvature (cylinder radius 572.5 mm on one face) and two edge notches that likely engage with the splined ring's teeth for location and retention. A layout tag confirms a 40-fold rotational arrangement of the slats about the Y-axis, centered at approximately y = −80.4 mm. Completing the assembly is a knob (60 mm wide, 81 mm tall) with a spherical upper dome and conical stem that appears to cap the hub. One part (3f1c184a, single instance) lacks geometric data and may serve as an additional connector or spacer in the hub region.

Parts:
- bushing_or_locating_plug: This axisymmetric solid measures 46 × 46 × 77.22 mm (aspect ratio ≈ 1.68) and features a main cylindrical body of radius 20.0 mm (44.22 mm tall) topped by a short rim at radius 23.0 mm (5 mm tall) and a central blind hole of diameter 24.0 mm and depth 10.0 mm opening from the top. The lower portion tapers via a conical section to a smaller-diameter base, creating a stepped annular transition that could serve as an axial stop. The blind bore could receive a shaft or pin, and the tapered end appears to facilitate guided insertion or press-fitting. This part likely functions as a locating bushing or guide plug.
- flanged_bushing: An axisymmetric flanged ring measuring 56.5 mm in outer diameter and 11.25 mm tall (aspect ratio ~5). A thin flange at the base (3.25 mm thick, 56.5 mm OD) steps up to a narrower cylindrical boss (44.25 mm OD, 8.0 mm tall) with a 40 mm-diameter central through-bore spanning the full height. The flat flange bottom and the boss outer surface appear to serve as mating interfaces — the flange likely seats against a flat surface while the boss could locate into a matching bore. This part likely functions as a flanged bushing or collar.
- knob: A solid axially symmetric body with a spherical top (roughly 30 mm radius) blending into a conical taper that ends at a small flat circular base, fitting within a 60 × 60 × 81 mm bounding box (aspect ratio 1.35). The geometry is extremely simple — only three faces (sphere, cone, plane) with no holes or cylindrical features. The sole flat base face (normal [0, −1, 0]) is the only candidate mating surface; no through-holes are present. This part likely serves as a knob or grip element, with the spherical portion appearing sized for hand contact and the conical stem potentially seating into a receiving socket.
- vane_slat (×40): This part is a very thin, elongated panel approximately 3.9 × 50 × 300 mm (aspect ratio ~77) with one gently curved broad face defined by a 572.5 mm radius cylindrical surface, producing a subtle convex bow. Two small notches with filleted corners (radii 15 mm and 5 mm) are located near the top and bottom of one straight edge, likely serving as locating or retention features. With no holes, no recorded contact pairs, and an instance count of 40 in the assembly, this part appears to function as a repeated vane or slat element that could slot into a surrounding frame structure via its notches.
- part: 
- splined_ring: A thin annular ring approximately 200 mm in outer diameter and only 3.9 mm thick (aspect ratio ~51.3), featuring roughly 40 evenly-spaced rectangular teeth around its outer edge at radius 100 mm and a smooth central through-bore of diameter ~160 mm. The two flat annular faces permit axial stacking, while the external teeth could engage a mating internally-toothed bore. No assembly contact pairs are recorded. This part appears likely to function as a splined ring or separator plate that could transmit torque through its external spline teeth.
- rod: A featureless solid cylinder 500 mm long with a 10 mm diameter (radius 5.0 mm), giving an aspect ratio of 50. It comprises a single cylindrical surface, two flat circular end caps, and no holes, steps, or other features. No contact pairs are recorded in the assembly. Its extreme slenderness and uniform cross-section suggest it likely serves as a plain rod, pin, or shaft.
- disc_plate: A flat circular disc 180 mm in diameter and 10 mm thick (aspect ratio 18.0) with a single central through-hole of 10 mm diameter penetrating its full thickness. The top and bottom faces are broad, parallel planes, and the outer rim is a smooth cylindrical surface at 90 mm radius. No assembly contact pairs are recorded, but the part is grounded. The central bore could permit a shaft or fastener to pass through, and the large flat faces appear to serve as potential mating surfaces, suggesting this part likely functions as a base disc or cover plate.

Annotation Caveats:
Part 3f1c184a (1 instance) has null extraction status and no geometric description, so its role is unknown. Contact-edge counts are entirely empty; no face-to-face contacts were recorded in the assembly graph, meaning all inter-part relationships are inferred from hole edges and geometric correspondence alone. The rod-to-disc-plate hole link shows only 0.3 mm axial overlap relative to a 10 mm hole length, which may indicate the rod is nearly flush or the overlap detection is marginal. The matched_radius_mean of 23 mm in the bushing-to-plug hole edge exceeds the bore's nominal 20 mm radius, suggesting the plug's 23 mm rim shoulder was matched rather than the main body cylinder.
